name: Biological Cell
description: The fundamental unit of life, capable of replication and maintaining complex organized systems.

name: Hydrogen Atom
description: The simplest and most abundant element in the universe, fueling stars and cosmic expansion.

name: Byte
description: A unit of digital information, representing logical truth values and structured data.

name: Script
description: A sequence of logical instructions determining the flow and operational rules of a subsystem.

name: Photon
description: A quantum of light and all other forms of electromagnetic radiation.

name: Abstract Thought
description: An emergent property of complex neural networks, capable of imagining entities that do not physically exist.

name: DNA Sequence
description: The universal information carrier holding the instructions for life's architecture.

name: Information Entropy
description: The measure of unpredictability and chaos within a structured system.

name: Proton
description: A subatomic particle with positive charge, constituent of atomic nuclei and essential for matter formation in the nuclear realm.

name: Neutron
description: A subatomic particle with neutral charge, stabilizing atomic nuclei and enabling nuclear fusion processes in stars.

name: Electron
description: A negatively charged subatomic particle orbiting nuclei, governing chemical bonding and electromagnetic interactions.

name: Meson
description: A composite subatomic particle composed of quark-antiquark pairs, mediating the strong nuclear force that binds nucleons.

name: Quark
description: Fundamental fermion particles that form composite hadrons through strong interactions, never existing as isolated entities.

name: Nucleon
description: A collective term for protons and neutrons forming the atomic nucleus and determining the mass and stability of atoms.

name: Protein
description: A complex biomolecule composed of amino acids, serving as enzymes, structural components, and regulatory molecules in all living systems.

name: Enzyme
description: A specialized protein that catalyzes biochemical reactions, enabling metabolic processes and life sustenance at cellular level.

name: Ribosome
description: A cellular organelle synthesizing proteins from mRNA instructions, bridging genetic information with functional protein production.

name: Mitochondrion
description: The energy-producing organelle in cells, converting nutrients into ATP and powering all cellular functions and life processes.

name: Algorithm
description: A step-by-step computational procedure for solving problems, forming the logical foundation of all software and artificial intelligence systems.

name: Data Structure
description: An organized format for storing and accessing data efficiently, determining the performance and scalability of computational systems.

name: Network Protocol
description: A set of rules enabling communication between computers and devices, forming the foundation of the interconnected digital ecosystem.

name: Application Programming Interface (API)
description: An interface allowing different software systems to communicate, enabling integration, modularity, and ecosystem interconnection.

name: Consciousness
description: The emergent phenomenon of self-awareness arising from neural complexity, forming the basis of perception, decision-making, and existence.

name: Electromagnetic Wave
description: A traveling oscillation of electric and magnetic fields carrying energy across space, ranging from radio waves to gamma rays.
